questions.question: What are the different types of variables in Java? Explain each type with an example.
keyPoints: Primitive types (byte, short, int, long, float, double, char, boolean)
Reference types (objects, instances of classes, interfaces, or arrays)
Local variables (declared inside a method or block, accessible within that scope)
Instance variables (declared inside a class, associated with an instance of that class)
Static variables (declared inside a class, shared by all instances of that class)

questions.question: How do you declare and initialize variables in Java? Provide examples.
Explain the concept of type conversion and casting in Java. Provide examples of automatic type promotion and explicit casting.
keyPoints: Automatic type promotion: converting a smaller type to a larger type
Explicit casting: converting a larger type to a smaller type

questions.question: What is the difference between a local variable and an instance variable in Java? Provide an example of each.
keyPoints: Local variable: declared inside a method or block, accessible within that scope
Instance variable: declared inside a class, associated with an instance of that class
Example of local variable: `int x = 10;` inside a method
Example of instance variable: `int x;` inside a class

questions.question: Explain the concept of static variables in Java. Provide an example of how to declare and use a static variable.
keyPoints: Static variable: declared inside a class, shared by all instances of that class
Example: `static int x = 10;` inside a class
